message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The number of persons daily infected with Covid-19 as a function of time is fitted with a trend line based on an iterative power law (
                  <jats:italic>n</jats:italic>
                  = ¼) with a day-to-day reproduction rate modelled with a polyline. From the trend line, an effective reproduction rate
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  of Covid-19 is calculated. In all three states,
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  decreases in the initial phase to one indicating that there is no exponential growth. In Sweden, a steady state with
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  around 1 and a high daily infection rates. In Germany,
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  = 1 is reached before public and private life is restricted. With these restrictions,
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  is reduced further to 0.87 (CI95 [0.83.; 0.91]) after 40 days so that, speculatively estimated, 9500 premature fatalities within two months may have been avoided. In France, it seems that only strongly restricting private life sends
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  down to 1 and further down to about 0.7 (CI95 [0.3; 1.1]) after 45 days. With
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  permanently below 1, an exponential decline of the number of daily infections is observed in Germany and France.
                </jats:p>
title: Modelling daily infections with Covid-19 in Germany, France, and Sweden with a trend line based on day-to-day reproduction rates

reference.unstructured: ARS / Santé Publique France / Ministère des Solidarités/Santé, Nombre de nouveau cas quotidiens de Covid-19 confirmés, 14 mai 2020
Böhmer M.M. et al., Lancet Infect Dis 2020, published online May 15, 2020
Mergel, D ., Physik mit Excel und Visual Basic, Springer Spektrum 2017, ISBN 978-3-642-37856-0, Chapter 9, Anpassung von Trendkurven an Messpunkte (Fitting trend curves to measurement data).
